json_entry.title: When anger overtakes you
json_entry.translation: I seek refuge with Allah from Satan.
json_entry.when_to_recite: When feeling rage rising or witnessing someone in anger

ledger_row: | anger-1 | anger | Sahih al-Bukhari 3282 | sahih | Sahih al-Bukhari (sunnah.com) | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Arabic and English phrase verified from sunnah.com

notes: Seed hadith specified in task. Context: Prophet ﷺ said, 'I know a word...' The dua text in this hadith is أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ, without الرَّجِيمِ; catalog wording now matches Bukhari 3282.

json_entry.title: Seeking refuge from Satan's provocation
json_entry.arabic: رَبِّ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ هَمَزَٰتِ ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينِ وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ رَبِّ أَن يَحْضُرُونِ
json_entry.transliteration: Rabbi a'udhu bika min hamazati-sh-shayatini wa-a'udhu bika rabbi an yahdhurun
json_entry.translation: My Lord! I seek refuge in You from the temptations of the devils. And I seek refuge in You, my Lord, that they even come near me.
json_entry.when_to_recite: When feeling provoked, stirred to anger, or sensing evil whispers

ledger_row: | anger-2 | anger | Quran 23:97-98 | N/A (Quran) | Dr. Mustafa Khattab, The Clear Quran (quran.com default) | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Translation 23:97 verbatim from quran.com (Clear Quran translation)
Translation 23:98 verbatim from quran.com (Clear Quran translation)

notes: Quranic duas require no grading. Translation is Dr. Mustafa Khattab's Clear Quran as served by quran.com default. Two consecutive verses joined as one dua per classical tafsir practice — they form a single supplication unit.

json_entry.title: When the devil provokes your heart
json_entry.arabic: وَإِمَّا يَنزَغَنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ نَزْغٌۭ فَٱسْتَعِذْ بِٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
json_entry.transliteration: Wa-imma yanazghannaka mina-sh-shaytani nazghun fa-sta'idh billah, innahu Sami'un 'Alim
json_entry.translation: If you are tempted by Satan, then seek refuge with Allah. Surely He is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
json_entry.when_to_recite: When feeling irritation, provocation, or a sudden surge of anger

ledger_row: | anger-3 | anger | Quran 7:200 | N/A (Quran) | Dr. Mustafa Khattab, The Clear Quran (quran.com default) | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Arabic + English translation verbatim from quran.com

notes: Quranic verse commanding the believer to seek refuge with Allah when Shaytan provokes. The verse itself contains both the description and the remedy. Commonly cited in fiqh of anger alongside Bukhari 3282.

json_entry.title: Protection from the evil eye
json_entry.arabic: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّةِ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْطَانٍ وَهَامَّةٍ وَمِنْ كُلِّ عَيْنٍ لاَمَّةٍ
json_entry.transliteration: A'udhu bi-kalimat-Allahi-t-tammati min kulli shaytanin wa hammatin wa-min kulli 'aynin lammah
json_entry.translation: I seek refuge in the Perfect Words of Allah from every devil, every harmful creature, and every harmful evil eye.
json_entry.when_to_recite: When seeking protection for yourself, your children, or loved ones from envy or the evil eye. The Prophet ﷺ used this supplication over Hasan and Husayn.

ledger_row: | envy-1 | envy | Sunan Ibn Majah 3525 | sahih | Darussalam (sunnah.com) | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Arabic text verified from sunnah.com; English adapted from the hadith context for reusable supplication
Grade: Sahih per Darussalam, confirmed on sunnah.com page

notes: Prophet ﷺ recited this over Hasan and Husayn, tracing the practice to Ibrahim ﷺ. The Arabic remains the hadith wording; the English no longer says 'for you both' so the UI can guide self, child, or loved-one use without implying the Arabic itself is dual.

json_entry.title: Protection from all harm through Allah's perfect words
json_entry.arabic: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
json_entry.transliteration: A'udhu bi-kalimat-Allahi-t-tammati min sharri ma khalaq
json_entry.translation: I seek refuge in the Perfect Words of Allah from the evil of that which He has created.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Morning and evening, and whenever fearing envy, harm, or malice from others

ledger_row: | envy-2 | envy | Sunan Ibn Majah 3518 | sahih | Darussalam (sunnah.com) | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Grade: Sahih per Darussalam, confirmed on sunnah.com page

notes: Broad protective dua covering all created evils, including hasad. Also recorded in Sahih Muslim 2708; the wording is well-attested. Recommended in morning/evening adhkar by classical scholars.

json_entry.title: Asking Allah to purify your heart from bitterness toward others
json_entry.arabic: رَبَّنَا ٱغْفِرْ لَنَا وَلِإِخْوَٰنِنَا ٱلَّذِينَ سَبَقُونَا بِٱلْإِيمَـٰنِ وَلَا تَجْعَلْ فِى قُلُوبِنَا غِلًّۭا لِّلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ رَبَّنَآ إِنَّكَ رَءُوفٌۭ رَّحِيمٌ
json_entry.transliteration: Rabbana-ghfir lana wa-li-ikhwaninallladhina sabaquna bi-l-imani wa-la taj'al fi qulubina ghillan lilladhina amanu rabbana innaka Ra'ufun Rahim
json_entry.translation: Our Lord! Forgive us and our fellow believers who preceded us in faith, and do not allow bitterness into our hearts towards those who believe. Our Lord! Indeed, You are Ever Gracious, Most Merciful.
json_entry.when_to_recite: When sensing jealousy or resentment stirring in the heart toward another believer

ledger_row: | envy-3 | envy | Quran 59:10 | N/A (Quran) | Dr. Mustafa Khattab, The Clear Quran (quran.com default) | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |
notes: The Arabic word 'ghillan' (غِلًّا) means rancor, bitterness, ill-will — which encompasses envy and resentment toward others. This dua asks Allah to remove that disease from the heart, making it ideal for the envy category. Quranic verse; no grading needed.

json_entry.title: Asking Allah for chastity and contentment
json_entry.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْهُدَى وَالتُّقَى وَالْعَفَافَ وَالْغِنَى
json_entry.transliteration: Allahumma inni as'aluka-l-huda wa-t-tuqa wa-l-'afafa wa-l-ghina
json_entry.translation: O Allah, I beg of Thee the right guidance, safeguard against evils, chastity and freedom from want.
json_entry.when_to_recite: As a daily supplication, especially when struggling with desire or temptation

ledger_row: | lust-1 | lust | Sahih Muslim 2721a | sahih | Sahih Muslim (sunnah.com) | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Grade: Sahih (Sahih Muslim collection; confirmed on sunnah.com page)

notes: The word 'afaf (عَفَاف) means chastity, moral purity, abstention from what is forbidden. This is a four-part dua asking for guidance, piety, chastity, and contentment — making it a comprehensive prophylactic against temptation. Narrated by 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud.

json_entry.title: Yusuf's prayer for protection from temptation
json_entry.arabic: قَالَ رَبِّ ٱلسِّجْنُ أَحَبُّ إِلَىَّ مِمَّا يَدْعُونَنِىٓ إِلَيْهِ ۖ وَإِلَّا تَصْرِفْ عَنِّى كَيْدَهُنَّ أَصْبُ إِلَيْهِنَّ وَأَكُن مِّنَ ٱلْجَـٰهِلِينَ
json_entry.transliteration: Rabbi-s-sijnu ahabbu ilayya mimma yad'unani ilayhi wa-illa tasrif 'anni kaydahunna asbu ilayhinna wa-akun mina-l-jahilin
json_entry.translation: My Lord! I would rather be in jail than do what they invite me to. And if You do not turn their cunning away from me, I might yield to them and fall into ignorance.
json_entry.when_to_recite: When facing sexual temptation or pressure to sin against one's own chastity

ledger_row: | lust-2 | lust | Quran 12:33 | N/A (Quran) | Dr. Mustafa Khattab, The Clear Quran (quran.com default) | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |
notes: The supplication of Prophet Yusuf ﷺ when tempted by Zulaykhah and the women of the city. One of the most powerful Quranic duas for protection from unlawful desire. The word 'asbu' (أَصْبُ) means 'I will incline/yield' — Yusuf's honesty before Allah about his human vulnerability is itself a model of tawakkul. Quranic verse; no grading needed.

json_entry.title: Seeking refuge from the evil of one's own soul
json_entry.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ فَاطِرَ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالأَرْضِ عَالِمَ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ رَبَّ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ وَمَلِيكَهُ أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ نَفْسِي وَشَرِّ الشَّيْطَانِ وَشِرْكِهِ
json_entry.transliteration: Allahumma Fatira-s-samawati wa-l-ardi 'Alima-l-ghaybi wa-sh-shahadati Rabba kulli shay'in wa-malikahu, ash-hadu an la ilaha illa ant, a'udhu bika min sharri nafsi wa-sharri-sh-shaytani wa-shirkihi
json_entry.translation: O Allah, Creator of the heavens and the earth, Who knowest the unseen and the seen, Lord and Possessor of everything. I testify that there is no god but Thee; I seek refuge in Thee from the evil within myself, from the evil of the devil, and his incitement to attributing partners to Allah.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Morning, evening, and before sleep — especially when struggling with desires of the lower self (nafs)

ledger_row: | lust-3 | lust | Sunan Abi Dawud 5067 | sahih | Al-Albani (sunnah.com) | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Grade: Sahih per Al-Albani, confirmed on sunnah.com page

notes: Grade is Sahih per Al-Albani (not Darussalam; sunnah.com uses Al-Albani's classification for Abu Dawud). The phrase 'sharri nafsi' (evil of my own soul) directly addresses the root cause of lustful temptation — the nafs al-ammara bi-s-su' (soul commanding to evil, cf. Quran 12:53). Recommended for morning, evening, and before sleep.

json_entry.title: The Master Supplication for Forgiveness (Sayyid al-Istighfar)
json_entry.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّي لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ خَلَقْتَنِي وَأَنَا عَبْدُكَ وَأَنَا عَلَى عَهْدِكَ وَوَعْدِكَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا صَنَعْتُ أَبُوءُ لَكَ بِنِعْمَتِكَ عَلَىَّ وَأَبُوءُ لَكَ بِذَنْبِي فَاغْفِرْ لِي فَإِنَّهُ لاَ يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ
json_entry.transliteration: Allahumma anta Rabbi la ilaha illa ant, khalaqtani wa-ana 'abduk, wa-ana 'ala 'ahdika wa-wa'dika ma-stata't, a'udhu bika min sharri ma sana't, abu'u laka bi-ni'matika 'alayya wa-abu'u laka bi-dhanbi fa-ghfir li fa-innahu la yaghfiru-dh-dhunuba illa ant
json_entry.translation: O Allah, You are my Lord, there is none worthy of worship except You. You have created me, and I am Your servant, and I am faithful to Your covenant and promise as much as I can. I seek refuge in You from the evil of what I have done. I acknowledge Your blessings upon me, and I admit my sins. So forgive me, for none forgives sins except You.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Morning and evening; after any sin; when feeling shame, regret, or a crushed heart

ledger_row: | shame-1 | shame | Sahih al-Bukhari 6306 | sahih | Sahih al-Bukhari (sunnah.com) | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Grade: Sahih (Sahih al-Bukhari; confirmed on sunnah.com page)

notes: The Prophet ﷺ called this 'Sayyid al-Istighfar' — the Master Supplication for Forgiveness. He said whoever recites it in the morning with conviction and dies that day enters Paradise, and similarly for evening. One of the most powerful duas for shame and repentance in the entire Sunnah.

json_entry.title: Adam and Hawwa's prayer after transgression
json_entry.arabic: قَالَا رَبَّنَا ظَلَمۡنَآ أَنفُسَنَا وَإِن لَّمۡ تَغۡفِرۡ لَنَا وَتَرۡحَمۡنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلۡخَٰسِرِينَ
json_entry.transliteration: Rabbana zalamna anfusana wa-in lam taghfir lana wa-tarhamna lanakunanna mina-l-khasirin
json_entry.translation: Our Lord! We have wronged ourselves. If You do not forgive us and have mercy on us, we will certainly be losers.
json_entry.when_to_recite: After any sin or mistake; when overwhelmed with shame; when seeking Allah's mercy after wronging oneself

ledger_row: | shame-2 | shame | Quran 7:23 | N/A (Quran) | Sahih International (quran.com) | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |
notes: The first human prayer of repentance recorded in the Quran — Adam and Hawwa after they ate from the forbidden tree. The quran.com page showed 'Sahih International' labeling on this verse. A profound model of acknowledging sin without despair. Quranic verse; no grading needed.

json_entry.title: Asking for pardon, forgiveness, and mercy
json_entry.arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تُؤَاخِذْنَآ إِن نَّسِينَآ أَوْ أَخْطَأْنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا وَلَا تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنَآ إِصْرًۭا كَمَا حَمَلْتَهُۥ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا وَلَا تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِۦ ۖ وَٱعۡفُ عَنَّا وَٱغۡفِرۡ لَنَا وَٱرۡحَمۡنَآۚ أَنتَ مَوۡلَىٰنَا فَٱنصُرۡنَا عَلَى ٱلۡقَوۡمِ ٱلۡكَٰفِرِينَ
json_entry.transliteration: Rabbana la tu'akhidhna in nasina aw akhta'na, rabbana wa-la tahmil 'alayna isran kama hamaltahu 'ala-lladhina min qablina, rabbana wa-la tuhammilna ma la taqata lana bih, wa-'fu 'anna wa-ghfir lana wa-rhamna, anta mawlana fa-nsurna 'ala-l-qawmi-l-kafirin
json_entry.translation: Our Lord! Do not punish us if we forget or make a mistake. Our Lord! Do not place a burden on us like the one you placed on those before us. Our Lord! Do not burden us with what we cannot bear. Pardon us, forgive us, and have mercy on us. You are our only Guardian. So grant us victory over the disbelieving people.
json_entry.when_to_recite: When carrying shame or guilt from past mistakes; before sleep; when seeking comprehensive forgiveness

ledger_row: | shame-3 | shame | Quran 2:286 | N/A (Quran) | Dr. Mustafa Khattab, The Clear Quran (quran.com default) | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |
notes: The closing dua of Surah al-Baqarah. The Prophet ﷺ said these two final verses were given directly from beneath the Throne of Allah (Sahih Muslim 806). The supplication encompasses forgetfulness, mistakes, unbearable burdens, and culminates in a triple request: pardon ('afw), forgiveness (maghfirah), mercy (rahmah) — addressing shame at all three levels. Quranic verse; no grading needed.

json_entry.title: Crying out to the Ever-Living in distress
json_entry.arabic: يَا حَىُّ يَا قَيُّومُ بِرَحْمَتِكَ أَسْتَغِيثُ
json_entry.transliteration: Ya Hayyu Ya Qayyumu bi-rahmatika astaghith
json_entry.translation: O Ever-Living, O Self-Sustaining Sustainer! In Your Mercy do I seek relief.
json_entry.when_to_recite: In moments of despair, spiritual heaviness, or shame that feels unbearable

ledger_row: | shame-4 | shame | Jami' at-Tirmidhi 3524 | hasan | Darussalam (sunnah.com) | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Grade: Hasan per Darussalam, confirmed on sunnah.com page

notes: Narrated by Anas ibn Malik: the Prophet ﷺ recited this when troubled by difficult matters. Ya Hayyu Ya Qayyum are two of Allah's greatest names. The word 'astaghith' means to cry out for help/rescue — especially appropriate for the shame category where someone feels trapped and unable to rise.
